revision.comment: 新しいページ：狐井塚古墳（奈良県大和高田市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''狐井塚古墳'''（きついづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和郡山市]]池田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''陵西陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]][[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の南側に平行して築造された古墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘表面からの出土遺物としては[[埴輪]]がある。墳丘周囲には周濠がめぐらされており、現在も溜池・水田として遺存する{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでない。池田集落にある地蔵堂には兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。（竜山石）製の[[長持形石棺]]蓋石を転用した石棺仏があるほか、北西の春日神社境内には長持形石棺の底石があり、これらを本古墳のものとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また周辺には古墳数基が分布しており、そのうち5基が[[宮内庁]]によって[[陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 この狐井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期後半の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定され、築山古墳よりも新しい時期に位置づけられる{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]]の[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]（[[仁賢天皇]]2年死去）の墓とする説があり、現在では[[宮内庁]]により陵墓参考地に治定されている。 == 構造 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：75メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}（または74メートル{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}） * 後円部 ** 直径：45メートル ** 高さ：6メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル == 陪塚 == 宮内庁によって治定されている陪冢（[[陪塚]]）は次の5基。 * 飛地い号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径6メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地ろ号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径13メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地は号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径15メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地に号（大和高田市敷島町） - 円墳、直径15メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳、大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径20メートル。周濠を有する。出土品として埴輪（円筒形・家形・盾形・靫形）・[[須恵器]]がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|飛地い号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|飛地ろ号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|飛地は号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|飛地に号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=狐井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|chapter=|year=1996|title=大和高田市遺跡分布調査報告書I（大和高田市文化財調査報告5）|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[難波小野王]] * [[築山古墳 (大和高田市)]]（磐園陵墓参考地） {{DEFAULTSORT:きついつかこふん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:大和郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''狐井塚古墳'''（きついづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''陵西陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]][[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の南側に平行して築造された古墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘表面からの出土遺物としては[[埴輪]]がある。墳丘周囲には周濠がめぐらされており、現在も溜池・水田として遺存する{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでない。池田集落にある地蔵堂には兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。（竜山石）製の[[長持形石棺]]蓋石を転用した石棺仏があるほか、北西の春日神社境内には長持形石棺の底石があり、これらを本古墳のものとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また周辺には古墳数基が分布しており、そのうち5基が[[宮内庁]]によって[[陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 この狐井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期後半の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定され、築山古墳よりも新しい時期に位置づけられる{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]]の[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]（[[仁賢天皇]]2年死去）の墓とする説があり、現在では[[宮内庁]]により陵墓参考地に治定されている。 == 構造 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：75メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}（または74メートル{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}） * 後円部 ** 直径：45メートル ** 高さ：6メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル == 陪塚 == 宮内庁によって治定されている陪冢（[[陪塚]]）は次の5基。 * 飛地い号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径6メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地ろ号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径13メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地は号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径15メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地に号（大和高田市敷島町） - 円墳、直径15メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳、大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径20メートル。周濠を有する。出土品として埴輪（円筒形・家形・盾形・靫形）・[[須恵器]]がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|飛地い号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|飛地ろ号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|飛地は号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|飛地に号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=狐井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[河上邦彦]]|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|chapter=|year=1996|title=大和高田市遺跡分布調査報告書I（大和高田市文化財調査報告5）|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[難波小野王]] * [[築山古墳 (大和高田市)]]（磐園陵墓参考地） {{DEFAULTSORT:きついつかこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:大和高田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''狐井塚古墳'''（きついづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''陵西陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]][[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の南側に平行して築造された古墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘表面からの出土遺物としては[[埴輪]]がある。墳丘周囲には周濠がめぐらされており、現在も溜池・水田として遺存する{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。主体部の埋葬施設は明らかでない。池田集落にある地蔵堂には兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。（竜山石）製の[[長持形石棺]]蓋石を転用した石棺仏があるほか、北西の春日神社境内には長持形石棺の底石があり、これらを本古墳のものとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また周辺には古墳数基が分布しており、そのうち5基が[[宮内庁]]によって[[陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 この狐井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期後半の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定され、築山古墳よりも新しい時期に位置づけられる{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]]の[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]（[[仁賢天皇]]2年死去）の墓とする説があり、現在では[[宮内庁]]により陵墓参考地に治定されている。 == 構造 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 古墳の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：75メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}（または74メートル{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}） * 後円部 ** 直径：45メートル ** 高さ：6メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル == 陪塚 == 宮内庁によって治定されている陪冢（[[陪塚]]）は次の5基。 * 飛地い号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径6メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地ろ号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径13メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地は号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径15メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地に号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径15メートル以上。出土品として埴輪がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 * 飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳、大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径20メートル。周濠を有する。出土品として埴輪（円筒形・家形・盾形・靫形）・[[須恵器]]がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|飛地い号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|飛地ろ号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|飛地は号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|飛地に号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=狐井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|chapter=|year=1996|title=大和高田市遺跡分布調査報告書I（大和高田市文化財調査報告5）|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[難波小野王]] * [[築山古墳 (大和高田市)]]（磐園陵墓参考地） {{DEFAULTSORT:きついつかこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:大和高田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''狐井塚古墳'''（きついづかこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群のうち築山古墳群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''陵西陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]][[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]西部、馬見丘陵南端において[[築山古墳 (大和高田市)|築山古墳]]（磐園陵墓参考地）の南側に平行して築造された古墳である。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を東方向に向ける。墳丘表面からの出土遺物としては[[埴輪]]がある。墳丘周囲には周濠がめぐらされており、現在も溜池・水田として遺存する{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設は明らかでない。池田集落にある地蔵堂には兵庫県加古川流域産の成層[[ハイアロクラスタイト]]竜山石は、かつて流紋岩質溶結凝灰岩とされてきたが、近年の調査によって流紋岩質成層ハイアロクラスタイト（水冷破砕岩）と判明している（[https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html 宝殿石（竜山石）]（兵庫県ホームページ）参照）。（竜山石）製の[[長持形石棺]]蓋石を転用した石棺仏があるほか、北西の春日神社境内には長持形石棺の底石があり、これらを本古墳のものとする説が挙げられている{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。また周辺には古墳数基が分布しており、そのうち5基が[[宮内庁]]によって[[陪冢]]（陪塚）に治定されている。 この狐井塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]中期後半の[[5世紀]]後半頃の築造と推定され、築山古墳よりも新しい時期に位置づけられる{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、第23代[[顕宗天皇]][[皇后]]の[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]]（[[仁賢天皇]]2年死去）の墓とする説があり、現在では[[宮内庁]]により陵墓参考地に治定されている。 == 墳丘 == [[File:Tukiyama kofun 01.jpg|250px|thumb|right|{{center|築山古墳群}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}。}}]] 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：75メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}（または74メートル{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}） * 後円部 ** 直径：45メートル ** 高さ：6メートル{{Sfn|河上邦彦|2006}}{{Sfn|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * 前方部 ** 幅：40メートル == 陪塚 == 宮内庁によって治定されている陪冢（[[陪塚]]）は次の5基。 * 飛地い号（大和高田市池田） - 円墳、直径20メートル。周濠を有する。出土品として埴輪（円筒形・家形・盾形・靫形）・[[須恵器]]がある奈良県遺跡地図Web。。 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|飛地い号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|飛地ろ号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|飛地は号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|飛地に号 File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|飛地ほ号（領家山1号墳） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=狐井塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名 刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvid|狐井塚古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=河上邦彦|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=大和葛城の大古墳群 馬見古墳群（シリーズ「遺跡を学ぶ」026）|publisher=新泉社|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvid|河上邦彦|2006}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|chapter=|year=1996|title=大和高田市遺跡分布調査報告書I（大和高田市文化財調査報告5）|publisher=大和高田市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[難波小野王]] * [[築山古墳 (大和高田市)]]（磐園陵墓参考地） {{DEFAULTSORT:きついつかこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:大和高田市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

* [[Umami Kofun Group]]** {{ill|Kitsui Tsuka Kofun|ja|狐井塚古墳}} (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E7%8B%90%E4%BA%95%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] 2025

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Naniwa no Ono no Ōkimi|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Kensō Tennō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: * Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> * Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref> * Front rectangular portion: * Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/>
== Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: * Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> * Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref> * Front rectangular portion: * Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/>
== Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>

revision.text:
'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: * Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> * Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref> * Front rectangular portion: * Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/>
== Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: * Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> * Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref> * Front rectangular portion: * Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/>
== Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>
<gallery> File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) </gallery>

== Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical Names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Burial Mounds of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}}

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/> Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.<ref name="Ryuzan Stone">Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website).</ref> The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus.
Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency.
== Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map"/><ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref> (or 74 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> ** Height: 6 meters<ref>{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}</ref><ref>{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}</ref>
* Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map" /> == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref>

'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster).
Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant.
== Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date.
This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.<ref name="Nara Prefecture Map">Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.</ref> * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. 墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.
