{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 1]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paddies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei Mausoleum Reference Site'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buqqha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical Names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Burial Mounds of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}} * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono Mausoleum Reference Site) [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Imperial Mausoleum Reference Sites]] [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]

It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buqqha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|陵西陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buqqha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical Names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Burial Mounds of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}} * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s]] [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buqqha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical Names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Burial Mounds of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}} * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s]] [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buqqha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. 高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. 高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice paqqies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. 墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical Names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Kofuns of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}} * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}) [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Makimuku Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kitsuneizuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Imperial {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}s]] [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |別名 = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[大和高田市]]池田 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 16.05 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 43|経度秒 = 59.12 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |出土品 = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所}}}}]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 2]]'''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada City|ja|大和高田市}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|ja|難波小野王}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperror Kensō|ja|顕宗天皇}}, as a possible occupant. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)}} (designated as Iwazono {{ill|Mausoleum Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. Artifacts such as {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but artifacts like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style stone coffin|ja|長持形石棺|1=Nagamochi style stone sarcophagus}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyogo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style stone coffin also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are designated as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound Dimensions == The dimensions of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are designated as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. Artifacts include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological Map Web.
