But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Kofuns of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]] * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井塚古墳|version=93995613|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleum_Reference_Sites]] [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298212}}

It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. 墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. [[sn:commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yamatotakada|WD=Q838669}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.521125|N|135.7330888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q107298212]] Wikidata name: Kitsuizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Kofuns of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]] * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井塚古墳|version=93995613|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mausoleum_Reference_Sites]] [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada City]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298212}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. [[commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yamatotakada|WD=Q838669}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.521125|N|135.7330888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q107298212]] Wikidata name: Kitsuizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Kofuns of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]] * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. [[commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yamatotakada|WD=Q838669}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.521125|N|135.7330888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q107298212]] Wikidata name: Kitsuizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Kofuns of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]] * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. [[co:commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kitsuizuka Kofun]][[ja:狐井塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yamatotakada|WD=Q838669}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.521125|N|135.7330888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q107298212]] Wikidata name: Kitsuizuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 狐井塚古墳 |image = [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に前方部、右奥に後円部） |alternate_name = |所属 = [[馬見古墳群]]（南群うち築山古墳群） |location = [[:en:奈良県|奈良県]][[:en:大和高田市|大和高田市]]池田 |coordinates = 34|coordinates = 31|coordinates = 16.05 |coordinates =135|coordinates = 43|coordinates = 59.12 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[前方後円墳]] |area = 墳丘長75m
高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yamatotakada, Nara|Yamatotakada City]], Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]], consort of the 23rd Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kenzō|Emperror Kensō]], as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]].Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-1.jpg|Higote I Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-2.jpg|Higote Ro Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-3.jpg|Higote Ha Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-4.jpg|Higote Ni Kofun File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, baicho-5.jpg|Higote Ho Kofun (Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb) == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in this article:}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=Kitsuizuka Kofun|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]] 30, Nara Prefecture Geographical names (Post-publication Edition)|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490301|ref={{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Kawakami Kunihiko|authorlink=河上邦彦|year=2006|chapter=|title=Great Kofuns of Yamato Katsuragi: Makimuku Kofun Cluster (Series: Learning from Archaeological Sites 026)|publisher=Shinsensha|isbn=978-4787706362|ref={{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}}} == Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} * [[:en:Princess Naniwa no Ono|Princess Naniwa no Ono]] * {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}) * [[Umami Kofun Cluster|Umami Kofun Group]]''' (Yamatotakada, keyhole-shaped, total length 75 m). ... 6 KB (745 words) - 21:16, 15 January ''' {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|狐井塚古墳|version=93995613|comment=Imported full ja history}} s]] }} {{draft categories| [[ja:狐井塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Kitsuizuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q107298212}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:History of Yamatotakada City]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mausoleum_Reference_Sites]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada City}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|en|Princess Naniwa no Ono|WD=Q11659541|lt=Princess Naniwa no Ono}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|WD=Q360966|lt=Emperror Kensō}}, as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. 高さ6m（後円部） |Burial Facility = 不明（内部に[[長持形石棺]]?） |burial goods = [[埴輪]] |陪塚 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）5基 |built = [[5世紀]]後半 |Interred Persons = （[[宮内庁]]推定）[[難波小野王|難波小野女王]] |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「陵西陵墓参考地」 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-東北東 |ラベル = 狐井塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Kitsuizuka Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|The {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}}}]] '''Kitsuizuka Kofun''' (狐井塚古墳) is the name of a [[kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yamatotakada, Nara|en|Yamatotakada, Nara|WD=Q838669|lt=Yamatotakada City}}, Nara Prefecture. It is a key site within the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (southern group, Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster). Although the actual individual buried here remains unknown, it has been Historic Site1 by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as a "'''Ryōsei {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}'''" (陵西陵墓参考地), with {{ill|Princess Naniwa no Ono|en|Princess Naniwa no Ono|WD=Q11659541|lt=Princess Naniwa no Ono}}, consort of the 23rd Emperor {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|WD=Q360966|lt=Emperror Kensō}}, as a possible Interred_individual. == Overview == The Kitsuizuka Kofun is located at the southern end of the Makimuku Hill in the western Nara Basin, aligned parallel to the {{ill|Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|ja|築山古墳 (大和高田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukiyama Kofun (Yamatotakada)|qq=}} (Historic Site1 as Iwazono {{ill|Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}). No excavations have been conducted to date. This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun, oriented with the front square portion facing east. burial goods such as {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been discovered from the exterior of the mound. Surrounding the mound is a moat, parts of which remain as ponds and rice pa11ies today. The burial facilities remain unidentified, but burial goods like the lid of a {{ill|Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}, made from Ryuzan stone (Tatsuyama stone), were reused as a stone Buddha in the nearby Jizōdō hall.Recent studies identify Tatsuyama stone as rhyolitic hyaloclastite (hydrovolcanic rock), contrary to its previous identification as rhyolitic welded tuff ([https://web.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/sr09/jibasan/31.html Tatsuyama Stone], Hyōgo Prefecture website). The lower stone of a Nagamochi-style Sarcophagus also resides in the precincts of Kasuga Shrine, further suggesting its origin from this tumulus. Surrounding the Kitsuizuka Kofun are several associated kofun, five of which are Historic Site1 as {{ill|Baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baizuka|qq=}} (associated mounds) by the Imperial Household Agency. == Mound area == The area of the mound are as follows: * Total length: 75 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}} (or 74 meters{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}}) * Rear circular portion: ** Diameter: 45 meters ** Height: 6 meters{{Harvnb|Kawakami|2006}}{{Harvnb|Kitsuizuka Kofun (Heibonsha, Post-publication Edition)|2006}} * Front rectangular portion: ** Width: 40 meters == Associated Mounds == The following five kofun are Historic Site1 as Baizuka (陪塚) by the Imperial Household Agency: * Higote I Kofun (飛地い号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 6 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ro Kofun (飛地ろ号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 13 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ha Kofun (飛地は号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ni Kofun (飛地に号, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter over 15 meters. Haniwa fragments were found.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web. * Higote Ho Kofun (飛地ほ号, Ryoke Mountain 1st Tomb, Yamatotakada City, Ikeda) - Circular mound, diameter 20 meters, with a surrounding moat. burial goods include cylindrical, house-shaped, shield-shaped, and quiver-shaped haniwa, as well as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}.Nara Prefecture Archaeological map Web.
